1.name: Kneeling Push-Ups
1.tip: Reduce load, build form. Keep a straight line from knees to shoulders. Core stays tight throughout.

1.name: Chair Dips
1.tip: Use a sturdy chair. Feet closer to you = easier. Lower slowly, push up strong.

1.name: Bicep Curls (Backpack)
1.tip: Fill a backpack with books. Keep elbows pinned at sides. Full range of motion.

1.name: Incline Push-Ups
1.tip: Hands on a counter or couch. The higher the surface, the easier. Still keep core tight.

1.name: Resistance Band Curls
1.tip: Stand on the band, curl both arms up simultaneously. Controlled on the way down.

2.name: Standard Push-Ups
2.tip: Full plank position. Lower until chest nearly touches floor. Don't let hips sag or pike up.

2.name: Close-Grip Push-Ups
2.tip: Triceps emphasis. Hands shoulder-width or closer. Elbows track along your sides.

2.name: Kettlebell Floor Press
2.tip: Lie on your back. Press one or both KBs straight up. Control the descent.

2.name: Decline Push-Ups
2.tip: Feet elevated on a chair. More upper chest and shoulders. Keep body rigid.

2.name: Feet-Elevated Dips
2.tip: Chair behind you, feet on another chair. Lower slowly, push explosively.

2.name: Diamond Push-Ups
2.tip: Form a diamond shape with thumbs and index fingers. Intense tricep focus.

2.name: Pike Push-Ups
2.tip: Hips high in an upside-down V. Bend elbows and lower head toward the floor. Shoulder builder.

2.name: Kettlebell Shoulder Press
2.tip: Standing or seated. Press overhead, lock out at top. Control the lowering phase.

2.name: Chin-Ups
2.tip: Palms facing you. Bicep-dominant pull. Aim for full hang to chin-above-bar.

2.name: KB Tricep Extensions
2.tip: Hold KB overhead with both hands. Lower behind head slowly, extend back up.

3.name: Explosive Push-Ups / Clap Push-Ups
3.tip: Push hard enough to leave the ground. Land softly with slightly bent elbows. Builds power.

3.name: Archer Push-Ups
3.tip: Wide stance, shift weight to one arm as you lower. The other arm stays extended. Alternating.

3.name: Kettlebell Push-Up Rows
3.tip: One hand on KB. Push-up, then row that KB to hip. Alternate sides. Core fights rotation.

3.name: Bodyweight Dips
3.tip: Full range of motion. Lean slightly forward for chest focus, upright for triceps.

3.name: Deficit Push-Ups
3.tip: Hands on elevated objects (books, plates). Allows deeper range of motion. Tougher than it sounds.

3.name: Kettlebell Clean to Press
3.tip: One fluid movement: swing up to rack position, then press overhead. Explosive and technical.

3.name: Tension Push-Ups
3.tip: Hold at the bottom 5–30 seconds OR lower for 4–8 seconds and press up fast. Brutal.

3.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raises
3.tip: Slight bend in elbows, raise to shoulder height. Slow and controlled beats heavy and sloppy.

3.name: Upright Row
3.tip: Pull KB or dumbbell up close to your body, elbows flare out above hands.

4.name: Weighted Explosive Push-Ups
4.tip: Add a weighted vest to any Phase 3 push-up variation for increased resistance.

4.name: Weighted Dips
4.tip: Attach weight via a dip belt, weighted vest, or hold a KB between your legs.

4.name: Weighted Archer Push-Ups
4.tip: Wear a weighted vest. Each rep is far more demanding on the working arm.

1.name: Bodyweight Squats
1.tip: Feet shoulder-width apart. Sit back and down, chest up. Knee tracks over second toe.

1.name: Reverse Lunges
1.tip: Step back, not forward. More knee-friendly. Lower back knee toward the floor.

1.name: Glute Bridges
1.tip: Lie on back, feet flat. Drive hips up, squeeze glutes hard at the top. Hold 1 second.

1.name: Step-Ups
1.tip: Use a sturdy chair or small step. Drive through the front heel. Keep torso upright.

1.name: Calf Raises
1.tip: Use a wall for balance if needed. Full range: from heel drop to full tiptoe extension.

2.name: Goblet Squat (Kettlebell)
2.tip: Hold KB at chest height. Sit deep into the squat, elbows track inside knees at bottom.

2.name: Walking Lunges
2.tip: Step forward, lower the back knee. Push off the front foot to step through. Weight optional.

2.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
2.tip: One foot on the floor, the other extended. Drive hips up. Harder than it looks!

2.name: Bulgarian Split Squats
2.tip: Rear foot on a chair. Lower the back knee toward the floor. Front shin stays vertical.

2.name: Jump Squats
2.tip: Squat down to parallel, then explode up. Land softly with bent knees to absorb impact.

2.name: Jump Lunges
2.tip: Lunge down, jump and switch legs mid-air. Stay light on the landing.

2.name: Kettlebell Swings
2.tip: Hip hinge, not a squat. Drive the KB with your hips. Keep back flat throughout.

2.name: Around the World Reverse Lunges
2.tip: Step back, then side, then across in one sequence per leg. Great hip mobility work.

2.name: Hamstring Curls
2.tip: Lie prone, anchor feet under something heavy, and curl your heels toward your glutes.

3.name: Pistol Squat Progressions
3.tip: Start with box-assisted or holding a support. One leg straight out in front, lower on the other.

3.name: Kettlebell Swings (Heavy)
3.tip: Go heavier. Explosive hip snap. The KB should feel weightless at the top.

3.name: Weighted Step-Ups
3.tip: Hold a KB in each hand or wear a backpack. Drive through the heel of the working leg.

3.name: Romanian Deadlifts (RDL)
3.tip: KB or weight, hinge at hips, back flat. Feel the hamstring stretch, drive hips forward to stand.

3.name: Static Lunges with KB Pass-Through
3.tip: In a lunge, pass the KB under your front leg. Challenges balance and coordination.

3.name: Glute Bridges (Weighted)
3.tip: KB on hips. Drive hard. Control the descent. Heavier weight, higher the challenge.

3.name: KB Overhead Squats
3.tip: Press KB overhead, keep it locked, then squat deep. Demands shoulder mobility and core stability.

3.name: Bulgarian Split Squats (Weighted)
3.tip: Add a KB or two. More load, more burn. Keep front shin vertical and chest up.

3.name: Front Rack Squats
3.tip: KB in front rack position (forearm vertical, elbow high). Deep squat. Upright torso is key.

4.name: Weighted Pistol Squats
4.tip: Hold a KB while performing a pistol squat. Extreme single-leg strength.

4.name: Weighted Jump Squats
4.tip: Add a light KB or vest. Absorb carefully on landing.

1.name: Dead Bugs
1.tip: Press lower back into the floor. Extend opposite arm and leg slowly. Control your breathing.

1.name: Crunches
1.tip: Focus on contracting the abs, not pulling your neck. Short range of motion done right beats big swings done wrong.

1.name: Leg Raises (Floor)
1.tip: Hands under hips. Lower legs slowly toward the ground without touching. Keep low back pressed down.

1.name: Plank (20–30 sec)
1.tip: Forearms or hands. Straight line from head to heels. Squeeze everything: abs, glutes, thighs.

1.name: Penguins
1.tip: Lie on back, knees bent. Slide hands toward each ankle alternating side to side. Oblique focus.

2.name: V-Ups
2.tip: Legs and torso rise simultaneously to a V shape. Reach for your toes. Lower slowly.

2.name: Hanging Knee Raises
2.tip: From a bar, bring knees to chest. Control the swing. Squeeze abs at the top.

2.name: Side Planks (30–40 sec)
2.tip: Stack feet or stagger them. Keep hips up. Don't let them sag toward the floor.

2.name: Russian Twists (KB optional)
2.tip: Lean back 45°, feet up. Rotate side to side. Add a KB for more resistance.

2.name: Kettlebell Marches
2.tip: Hold a KB overhead, march in place with high knees. Core fights to stay stable and upright.

2.name: Plank Shoulder Taps
2.tip: In push-up plank, tap each shoulder alternately. Hips stay square — no rocking.

2.name: Mountain Climbers
2.tip: Drive knees to chest alternating as fast as you can maintain form. Great cardio + core combo.

2.name: Hollow Hold
2.tip: Press lower back to floor, arms and legs extended and hovering. Brutal for the entire core.

3.name: Hanging Leg Raises (Full)
3.tip: Straight legs from a dead hang to parallel or beyond. Requires serious control.

3.name: Toe-to-Bar Progressions
3.tip: Work from knee raises → L-raise → toe-to-bar. Build the compression strength progressively.

3.name: Plank Walk-Outs
3.tip: From standing, walk hands out to full plank, then walk back. Demands shoulder and core stability.

3.name: KB Sit-Up to Press
3.tip: Hold KB at chest, sit up and press overhead at the top. Controlled on the descent.

3.name: Weighted Hollow Hold
3.tip: Hold a light KB or weight plate in the hollow position. Even 5 extra seconds is brutal.

3.name: Halos (Kettlebell)
3.tip: Hold KB by horns, circle around your head. Keep elbows close. Move slowly, feel the shoulder and core work.

3.name: Half Kneeling Chop
3.tip: One knee down, rotate KB from high to low across the body. Anti-rotation for obliques.

3.name: Ab Slide / Roll-Outs
3.tip: From knees, roll out to full extension (arms straight), pull back in. One of the hardest ab exercises.

4.name: Weighted Hanging Leg Raises
4.tip: Add ankle weights. The increased lever arm makes this extremely challenging.

4.name: Weighted Ab Roll-Outs
4.tip: Wear a weighted vest while rolling out. Near-planche level core demand.

1.name: Supermans
1.tip: Lie face down, arms extended. Lift arms, chest and legs simultaneously. Hold at the top.

1.name: Backpack Bent-Over Rows
1.tip: Hinge at hips, back flat. Pull backpack toward lower chest. Squeeze shoulder blades together.

1.name: Reverse Snow Angels
1.tip: Lie face down, arms at sides. Sweep arms up overhead and back. Focuses on upper back and rear delts.

1.name: Doorway Isometric Pulls
1.tip: Stand in doorway, grip the frame, lean back and pull. Isometric contraction — no movement, all tension.

1.name: Band-Assisted Pull-Up Negatives
1.tip: Use a resistance band for support. Jump to the top and lower yourself as slowly as possible.

2.name: Single-Arm KB Rows
2.tip: One knee and hand on a surface for support. Pull KB to hip height. Big range, controlled negative.

2.name: Hip Hinge / Good Mornings
2.tip: Hinge from hips, back stays flat. Hamstrings and lower back. Feel the stretch, not the strain.

2.name: Back Extensions
2.tip: Over the edge of a couch or bench. Lower torso down and raise back up. Lower back and glutes.

2.name: Pull-Ups
2.tip: Overhand grip. Full dead hang at bottom, chin over bar at top. No kipping — strict form.

2.name: Chin-Ups
2.tip: Underhand grip, more bicep involvement. Same strict movement as pull-ups.

2.name: Inverted Doorway Rows
2.tip: Hook a towel around a sturdy handle or use rings. Lean back and row your body up. Scaleable.

2.name: Upright Rows
2.tip: Pull KB or barbell up close to body, elbows flare high. Upper traps and rear delts.

3.name: Kettlebell Renegade Rows
3.tip: Push-up position with both hands on KBs. Row one arm at a time. Core fights rotation.

3.name: Kettlebell Gorilla Rows
3.tip: Straddle two KBs on the floor. Alternate rows. Heavier load, explosive pull.

3.name: Explosive Hip Hinges (KB)
3.tip: Drive the KB through the hips with power. Think of it as throwing your hips forward — not squatting.

3.name: Weighted Supermans
3.tip: Hold a light KB overhead as you perform supermans. Back extensors work overtime.

3.name: Weighted Vest Pull-Ups
3.tip: Standard pull-ups with added load. Every pound counts when you're hanging.

3.name: Inverted Rows (Feet Elevated)
3.tip: Feet up on a chair while doing inverted rows. Body nearly horizontal — very challenging.

3.name: Archer Pull-Ups
3.tip: Wide grip, pull toward one hand while the other arm stays extended. Extreme lat isolation.

4.name: Weighted Renegade Rows
4.tip: Use heavier KBs. The stability demand on the core increases with weight.

4.name: Weighted Vest Chin-Ups
4.tip: Add load to your chin-up. The bicep and lat combination becomes extremely demanding.
